what_it_does: It is a first-run quality report for an interpretive ledger sweep over code and design substrates, summarizing coverage statistics, model attribution, field fill rates, generator-level findings, an accuracy audit, a re-run decision, and a fix worklist.
observations: 4,614 files carry a full interpretive layer across claude-ds, counterpart-design, and company scopes.
Three models are attributed with timestamps: kimi-k2.7-code:cloud (74%), codex, and opus.
8,073 symbols were processed and 78% have per-symbol descriptions.
Field fill rates are high for what_it_does, observations, novelty, summary_for_embedding, purpose_vs_actual, and concerns, but inputs and outputs are 0% filled.

Findings F1-F8 cover missing semantic edges, weak JS/TS symbol extraction, silent truncation of 162 files, dead/duplicate columns, unharvested suggested_fields, unsafe incremental refresh, and a proposed independent coverage audit.
The accuracy audit sampled 34 files across models and formats and found all descriptions faithful.
The report recommends a hybrid path: harvest semantic edges and suggested fields now, defer JS/TS and truncation handling to the next re-run.
standouts: The semantic edge layer is missing: proposed_edge_kinds are bare type names without targets, while frontmatter already contains concrete attachment, comment, and channel relationships that could be harvested into real edges (F1).
162 files were silently truncated at a 48,000-character cap that is far below the models' actual context windows; no truncation flag or window is persisted (F3).
Incremental refresh would discard the entire interpretive layer because carry-forward for unchanged source hashes is not implemented (F6).
The accuracy audit passed 34/34, with kimi outputs shown to be as disciplined as opus despite making up 74% of the output.
conventions: Generator-fix-first: every finding is judged by whether the Stage-1 interface needs it, and hand-patching outputs is avoided.
Findings are tagged with IDs (F1-F8), priority stars, and generator-level fixes rather than output-level edits.
Model outputs are attributed with model name and timestamp, emphasizing provenance.
Coverage and fill-rate metrics are reported honestly, including empty columns and a corrected earlier miscount.
The report ends with a decision frame and a concrete worklist.
concerns: The report records that an earlier count of truncated files was wrong (436 vs 162), indicating the extraction pipeline can emit misleading intermediate numbers.
inputs and outputs columns are defined but never filled, making them dead schema fields.
proposed_edge_kinds contain relationship type names but no targets, so they are not usable edges yet.
No truncation flag or window is recorded in the ledger, so partial reads are not discoverable from the data.
Incremental refresh is currently unsafe because interpretation is not carried forward for unchanged files.
notes: A generator-level quality post-mortem of the first interpretive ledger sweep, flagging semantic edges, truncation, JS/TS symbol extraction, and incremental carry-forward as interface-critical fixes.
questions: What are the exact requirements of the Stage-1 interface that prioritizes these findings?
What is the full content of the v2-full prompt used to produce the interpretive layer?
How is source_hash computed and where is it stored in the ledger?
What is the detailed design in LEDGER-ENRICHMENT-PLAN.md?
Which files are classified as pure data dumps that should be excluded or described differently?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to report first-run quality and identify generator-level fixes prioritized by Stage-1 interface need; it does that and adds an accuracy audit, a decision framework, and a worklist, so it matches its purpose.
apparent_themes: Fix the generator, not the generated output.
Trust but verify through ground-truth accuracy audits.
Safe incremental maintenance of the interpretive layer.
intention_signals: It is trying to convert a one-off interpretive sweep into a durable, auditable, interface-ready knowledge graph by diagnosing generator defects and proposing pipeline fixes rather than editing individual descriptions.
novelty: It treats the ranked list of proposed_edge_kinds as a recognition signal from the model about which relationship types the fused graph should make first-class, and it proposes harvesting frontmatter relationships into typed edges without requiring a model re-run.
contribution: A structured, evidence-based quality assessment that links coverage metrics, model attribution, accuracy audit results, and prioritized generator fixes, giving a fused system a clear repair roadmap for the interpretive ledger.
summary_for_embedding: First-run quality report for an interpretive ledger sweep of code and design files, documenting coverage, model attribution, field fill rates, generator-level findings, accuracy audit results, and a prioritized fix worklist.
intention_for_embedding: To evaluate the first interpretive ledger sweep, confirm description accuracy, and identify generator-level repairs needed before the ledger can safely support a Stage-1 interface.
